segments.text: Why do you suddenly crave a burger when you see a red logo? Or why the fast food joints are practically covered in crimson red? It's not just a coincidence. Red does make you hungry.
And here's the mind-blowing science. Our brains are evolved to spot red as a sign of survival. Ripe fruits, fresh meat,
high energy. France like KFC and McDonald's use that red color to stir your body into action. It increases your heart rate, triggers hormones like grin,
and gets your impulses firing. And here's an amazing fact. The ketchup and mustard theory is real. Red and yellow together makes you happy, excited, and
you would want to eat more. But red always doesn't make you hungry. When unhealthy foods are served in red plates, you actually eat less. Here, red
acts as a sign of danger that is stopping you to eat more. So, it's basically acting like a traffic light for your body. So, colors are basically
guiding your cravings. From fast food science to the plate color, they are actually controlling our appetite.
